gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol tph-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006600 (C. elegans tryptophan hydroxylase); ADF neuron node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol ser-6 resolved to WB:WBGene00021897 (C. elegans octopamine GPCR); AWB neuron node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol mod-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00003386 (C. elegans serotonin-gated chloride channel); URX neuron node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol tbh-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006541 (C. elegans tyramine beta-hydroxylase); RIC neuron node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol nhr-76 resolved to WB:WBGene00015497 (C. elegans nuclear hormone receptor); intestine node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/atgl1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol ATGL resolved to atgl-1 = WB:WBGene00015484 (C. elegans adipose triglyceride lipase); intestine node in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/molecular_f.snippet: tph-1 annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (IBA); rate-limiting enzyme of serotonin biosynthesis.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 annotated to serotonin biosynthetic process (IBA); produces the 5-HT signal in the ADF neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/occurs_in.snippet: tph-1 localizes to neuron projection (IBA); figure places this activity in the ADF neuron within the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/molecular_f.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine receptor activity (IDA); receives the octopamine signal in the AWB neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine signaling pathway (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/occurs_in.snippet: ser-6 GPCR localizes to plasma membrane (IBA); figure places this activity in the AWB neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/molecular_f.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity (IDA); serotonin-gated anion channel receptor.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IDA/IMP).

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/occurs_in.snippet: mod-1 channel localizes to plasma membrane (IDA); figure places this activity in the URX neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/molecular_f.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity (IMP/ISS/IBA); converts tyramine to octopamine.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to octopamine biosynthetic process (IMP); produces the octopamine signal in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/occurs_in.snippet: tbh-1 localizes to synapse (IDA); figure places this activity in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/molecular_.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to nuclear receptor activity (IBA); ligand-dependent DNA-binding transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IMP); TF endpoint driving atgl-1 expression in intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-76 localizes to nucleus (IEA/IBA); figure places this activity in the INTESTINE.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/atgl1/molecular_.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity (ISS/IBA); adipose triglyceride lipase driving lipolysis.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triglyceride catabolic process (IBA/IEA); figure draws ATGL arrow to 'beta-oxidation / Fat loss' (the downstream process this lipase drives).

gomodel:run-figure1-004/atgl1/occurs_in.snippet: atgl-1 localizes to lipid droplet (IDA/EXP); figure places this activity in the INTESTINE.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/has_output/.snippet: tph-1 serotonin biosynthetic process produces serotonin (5-HT, CHEBI:28790) — the '5-HT' signal labelled on the ADF->URX arrow.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/has_input/C.snippet: mod-1 has serotonin binding (IDA) and is a serotonin-gated channel; serotonin (CHEBI:28790) is its gating ligand input.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/has_input/C.snippet: tbh-1 tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity uses tyramine (CHEBI:15760) as substrate.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/has_output/.snippet: tbh-1 octopamine biosynthetic process produces octopamine (CHEBI:17134) — the 'Oct' signal labelled on the RIC->AWB arrow.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/has_input/C.snippet: ser-6 octopamine receptor activity binds octopamine (CHEBI:17134) as its ligand input.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/has_input/.snippet: Panel E draws a solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL inside the INTESTINE; atgl-1 is the transcriptional target gene of the nhr-76 nuclear receptor.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tph1/causal/gomo.snippet: Noble et al. 2013 (Cell Metab): integrated serotonin/octopamine circuit. tph-1 serotonin synthesis (ADF) indirectly positively regulates mod-1 serotonin-gated channel activity (URX) via the diffusible 5-HT signal (ChEBI:28790) labelled on the ADF->URX arrow in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/tbh1/causal/gomo.snippet: Noble et al. 2013: tbh-1 octopamine synthesis (RIC) indirectly positively regulates ser-6 octopamine receptor activity (AWB) via the diffusible octopamine signal (ChEBI:17134) labelled 'Oct' on the RIC->AWB arrow in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/mod1/causal/gomo.snippet: Noble et al. 2013: mod-1 serotonin signalling (URX) indirectly positively regulates tbh-1 octopamine production (RIC), linking the serotonin and octopamine arms; drawn as a dashed (indirect/modulatory) pointed arrow URX->RIC in Panel E.

Noble et al. 2013: the neuronal circuit directs release of an endocrine signal to control body fat. Compartment edge URX->INTESTINE 'endocrine signal' modeled as mod-1 neuronal activity causally upstream of, positive effect, the intestinal nhr-76 nuclear receptor (the most upstream intestinal activity); mechanism of the endocrine signal not molecularly resolved, hence causally_upstream_of.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/nhr76/causal/gom.snippet: Panel E draws a solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL inside INTESTINE. nhr-76 (DNA-binding nuclear receptor) indirectly positively regulates atgl-1 triacylglycerol lipase activity — indirect because transcription/translation of atgl-1 is the intervening module (TF->target gene). No paper confirming the specific nhr-76->atgl-1 transcriptional link was located, so flagged as figure-read for curator verification.

gomodel:run-figure1-004/ser6/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E shows a solid pointed arrow (vertical, left side) from AWB (ser-6) to ADF (tph-1), i.e. ser-6 octopamine signalling indirectly positively regulates tph-1 serotonin synthesis. Low-confidence (0.3) edge flagged as unconfirmed against the figure in verification — figure-read only, for curator to check.
